paper_title: Semantic Browsing: Controllable Diversity for Image Generation

problem_reconstruction.reformulated_task: Controlled Semantic Diversity — generating structured, navigable image galleries where each variation corresponds to a specific, user-understandable semantic decision, rather than stochastic sampling noise.

shortcomings_of_prior_work: Prior diversity methods (CADS, Particle Guidance, SGI, Contextual Repulsion) produce variations driven by stochastic effects, not explicit semantic factors
Users lack control over what aspects of the scene vary
Generated outputs collapse into a single semantic mode even when diverse seeds are used
Existing methods treat outputs as independent samples, ignoring structural relationships
why_new_formulation.unexploited_opportunity: Recent T2I models trained on elaborated captions decouple semantic decision-making from pixel generation, enabling precise text-level control — a capability prior diversity methods have not leveraged.
why_new_formulation.key_insight: Strong prompt adherence is not a limitation on diversity but an enabler for precise semantic control. Diversity can be induced at the text level by constructing structured prompt alternatives.

research_formula.output: A rooted tree $(V, E)$ where each node is a fully specified scene interpretation $s \in \mathcal{S}$, and edges represent atomic semantic constraints
objective_requirements: Semantic Structuring: Children share a common aspect
Heterogeneity: Each constraint instantiates the aspect uniquely
Plausibility: All constraints remain logically consistent with the prompt and prior constraints
research_formula.optimization_tradeoff: Maximize semantic diversity while maintaining image quality, prompt adherence, and hierarchical consistency

challenges.challenge: Identifying which aspects of a scene are flexible for modification without violating prompt intent
challenges.method: Explicitly distinguishes explicit specifications (enforced by prompt or constraints) from unconstrained details filled in by the VLM. Isolates mutable details {d_i} for modification.
challenges.verification: Ablation study shows Hierarchical Consistency drops from 0.87 to 0.82 without Context Analyst (Table 3)

challenges.challenge: Synthesizing low-level mutable details into high-level semantic aspects for structured exploration
challenges.method: Applies inductive reasoning to aggregate several low-level details into one high-level aspect. Evaluates candidate aspects for impact magnitude on narrative, layout, and style.
challenges.verification: Separating Brainstormer from Decision Maker improves global DINO distance by 7.2% (Table 2)

challenges.challenge: Selecting the most impactful semantic dimension and instantiating diverse alternatives
challenges.method: Evaluates prompt-dependent candidate aspects, selects one high-impact dimension a*, and formulates widely divergent semantic constraints {c_i} that offer distinct interpretations.
challenges.verification: Ablation shows unified Brainstormer+Decision Maker yields lower diversity (0.362 vs 0.389 global DINO mean)

challenges.challenge: Ensuring proposed variations maintain logical consistency with the original prompt and accumulated constraints
challenges.method: Validates proposed constraints against the prompt p and constraint trajectory Cs. Identifies contradictions. Refines candidate set into precise, executable instructions.
challenges.verification: VQAScore drops from 0.90 to 0.87 without Critic (Table 3)

challenges.challenge: Encoding complex scene semantics for fine-grained control
challenges.method: Fully specified scene interpretations S encoded as structured JSONs, allowing fine-grained control over objects, attributes, and global scene properties.
challenges.verification: Qualitative results (Figures 7-9) demonstrate fine-grained semantic control across diverse prompts

challenges.challenge: Constructing a navigable hierarchy where each branch explores one semantic dimension at a time
challenges.method: Each branching point corresponds to one semantic aspect. 'Preserve' branches propagate unchanged states, allowing exploration along other dimensions from the same parent.
challenges.verification: Semantic-Topological Correlation: monotonic increase in DINO distance from 0.168 (1 hop) to 0.452 (5 hops) (Figure 11)

key_design_decisions.rationale: Leverages strong prompt adherence of modern T2I models. Enables interpretable and controllable variations.
key_design_decisions.alternative_considered: Stochastic variation in latent space (prior work), post-hoc selection from large candidate pools
key_design_decisions.empirical_support: Outperforms all latent-level and post-hoc baselines in Vendi, DINO, and human preference

key_design_decisions.decision: Hierarchical tree over flat set generation
key_design_decisions.rationale: Provides structured navigation, semantic inheritance, and traceable variation paths.
key_design_decisions.empirical_support: Semantic-Topological Correlation confirms tree encodes meaningful relationships; hierarchical consistency score 0.87

key_design_decisions.decision: Multi-agent decomposition (4 agents) over single agent
key_design_decisions.rationale: Each requirement (Structuring, Heterogeneity, Plausibility) needs specialized reasoning. Single agent cannot balance all three simultaneously.
key_design_decisions.empirical_support: Unified agent yields 7.2% lower diversity; Context Analyst and Critic each have measurable contributions

key_design_decisions.rationale: Provides both fully automatic gallery generation and user-driven selective exploration.
key_design_decisions.empirical_support: Scaling ablation shows manageable tradeoffs between depth, width, and performance

novel_metrics.name: Semantic-Topological Correlation
novel_metrics.method: Pairwise DINO distance vs. graph distance (path length in hops)
novel_metrics.purpose: Validate Semantic Structuring — topological neighbors should be semantically close
novel_metrics.result: Monotonic increase from 0.168 (1 hop) to 0.452 (5 hops)

novel_metrics.name: Hierarchical Consistency
novel_metrics.method: LLM-as-a-judge measuring alignment between node and inherited constraints
novel_metrics.purpose: Validate Plausibility — generated nodes must respect ancestor constraints

human_evaluation.result: Dominates in Diversity across all comparisons; holds majority for Overall Preference
